  3. Christian Alshon

United States

Christian Alshon

Powerful modern offense with fast hand battles

Biography

Christian Alshon moved into pickleball shortly after a successful collegiate tennis career and quickly became known for power, athleticism, and highlight-level shot making. His profile is excellent for studying the modern offensive game: heavy drives, fast hands, tweener/ATP awareness, and how to pressure from transition.

Major skills

Power drivesFast countersErne/ATP awarenessAggressive doublesTwo-handed attacks

Career notes

  • PPA profile lists top-level results across doubles, mixed, and singles.
  • Pickleball.com notes University of Chicago NCAA Division III tennis championship background.
  • Paddletek Bantam TKO-C was designed with him.

Events

SinglesMen’s doublesMixed doubles

Paddle / sponsor line

Paddletek Bantam TKO-C / sponsored line

What to study

Watch how he creates offensive windows from transition.

What club players can learn

Three things to study in Christian Alshon's game: Power drives, Fast counters, Erne/ATP awareness. Watching how a top professional applies these in live points is one of the fastest ways to add the same patterns to your own play.

Takeaways for 3.0–4.0 club players

This profile is not only a biography. It is a study note for deciding what to watch in Christian Alshon's game. Most club players cannot copy pro speed or athleticism, but they can copy the timing, spacing, and risk choices behind the point.

Start with Power drives. Level improvement often begins with knowing which ball can be attacked and which ball should be kept low. Then watch Fast counters. Good players usually win the next shot before it arrives because their court position is already prepared.

Finally, study Erne/ATP awareness. In pressure points, elite players often choose repeatable pressure over low-percentage winners. Read the profile with that lens and it becomes a practical training guide rather than just a player page.

  • Copy: ready position, court spacing, low resets.
  • Avoid copying: pro-level attacks that your current consistency cannot support.
  • Practice link: choose one pattern and repeat it with a partner for ten minutes.
